design_philosophy: Information-dense, professional workstation aesthetic. Clean architecture, speed, clarity, large datasets. Think Microsoft Excel combined with modern SaaS scheduling software. Avoid marketing-page vibes, prioritize high tabular density.

typography.rules: Never use Inter or Roboto for headings. Keep typography tight and functional. Use JetBrains Mono for numeric or tabular data where alignment is crucial.

colors.rules: Monochrome dominance. Color is function only. 95% of UI should be monochrome, utilizing pure white background and off-white/gray surfaces to define layout boundaries. Contrast ratios must meet APCA standards.

layout_and_spacing.spacing_scale: Extremely dense. Prefer p-1, p-2, p-4. Drop generous spacing in favor of data density.
layout_and_spacing.borders: Expose the skeleton. Use 1px solid borders (border-border) for technical look. Use border-collapse on tables.
layout_and_spacing.border_radius: rounded-none or rounded-sm (max 4px). Avoid overly rounded, playful UI elements.
layout_and_spacing.surfaces: Grid Borders variant. Use light gray surfaces for sidebars and toolbars, pure white for content panes.

components.buttons: Square or rounded-sm. High contrast. Primary buttons: bg-primary text-white hover:bg-primary-hover. Use secondary buttons for utility actions.
components.cards: Go cardless or use strictly rectangular components with 1px border. No soft shadows. No glassmorphism in main data areas.
components.tables_and_grids: CRITICAL: Excel-like density. padding-y 4px (p-1), padding-x 8px (p-2). Sticky headers with solid background (bg-surface). 1px borders on all cells. Hover states on rows. Support keyboard navigation.
components.forms: High density forms. Small inputs (h-8), small labels. Left-aligned inputs.
components.navigation: Sidebar with condensed links, Lucide icons on left. Top ribbon/toolbar for contextual actions, similar to Excel.
components.modals_drawers: Use sliding drawers for complex edits without leaving the grid context. Use modals strictly for alerts or short inputs.
components.icons: Use lucide-react. Keep sizes small (w-4 h-4) to match dense typography.

motion.rules: Minimalist micro-interactions. Fast transitions (150ms). No bouncy or slow animations. Hover states on all interactive elements. Data editing should feel instantaneous.

accessibility_and_testing.testing_attributes: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute in kebab-case, role-based (e.g., data-testid='import-wizard-next-button', data-testid='timetable-cell-1-1').
accessibility_and_testing.focus_states: High contrast focus rings. focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary focus:ring-offset-2.

image_urls.description: Elegant portrait of a young male professional.
Portrait of a confident Asian man adjusting tie.

media.rules: Use Lucide React icons for the application logo (e.g., Grid, Calendar, or Table icon). Avoid image-based logos. Use provided avatars for logged-in users.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build the UI focusing heavily on architecture, state management, and spreadsheet-like interactions.
Ensure all components use .js (JavaScript) instead of .tsx (TypeScript).
Use Shadcn UI extensively but override default margins/paddings to make it look dense, like an Excel workstation.
Implement horizontal and vertical scrolling for the Timetable Editor grid, with sticky row/column headers.
Use pure HTML table or a specialized grid component (like a highly customized div grid with standard borders) for the Timetable Editor.
Implement the 'Validation Center' as a persistent dock or side panel that updates when master data changes.
Implement 'data-testid' on every button, input, table cell, and navigation link.
